Job Title: Java Engineer - AI x Quality Engineering
Ad Text:
A major technology‑driven organisation is seeking a Senior Backend Java Engineer to join a specialised AI engineering team. You'll build intelligent backend agents that analyse code quality, generate tests, and enhance developer productivity. Ideal for engineers who enjoy deep technical work and presenting outcomes to senior stakeholders.

We are supporting a technology‑focused organisation investing heavily in AI‑enabled engineering capabilities. A specialised team is expanding its work on intelligent backend agents designed to support developers and uplift software quality across the organisation.

This high‑performing team of engineers owns the AI‑driven quality engineering strategy. They are now seeking a Senior Backend Java Engineer who can deliver robust backend systems while also representing the team's work to senior engineering leadership.

You will design and build backend components for AI‑powered agents that integrate directly into development pipelines. These agents analyse code quality, provide real‑time feedback, and automatically generate and execute unit tests. Your work will directly influence developer productivity and software reliability.

You'll collaborate closely with AI specialists, platform engineers, and senior technical leaders to deliver production‑ready backend solutions that support the organisation's broader engineering transformation.

Responsibilities:

Develop backend systems using Java, with supporting work in Python for AI‑related components
Build and enhance backend logic for code‑quality agents integrated into GitHub‑based CI/CD pipelines
Implement automated test generation and execution capabilities
Work closely with AI engineers to refine agent behaviour and decision‑making
Present technical outcomes and roadmap updates to senior engineering stakeholders
Contribute to broader AI engineering and developer‑experience initiatives
Ensure backend solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with engineering best practices

Skills & Experience:

Strong commercial experience in Java backend development
Proficiency in Python, ideally within AI, ML, or agent‑based systems
Experience developing or integrating AI agents, autonomous systems, or similar backend technologies
Experience building backend tools or platforms for developer productivity or code quality
Strong understanding of CI/CD pipelines, ideally GitHub Actions
Solid testing background, including:
JUnit (highly preferred)
Playwright, REST Assured, or Karate (advantageous)

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply. To apply please click apply or call Paolo Paparo on 02 •••• •150 for a confidential discussion.

About the Role

Take charge of AWS

MVSI is seeking a highly skilled Cloud Engineer to manage, optimise and scale the global AWS platform powering OnBoard, our world-class SaaS merchant onboarding solution trusted by enterprise customers worldwide.

This is an opportunity to work on a mission-critical platform operating across APAC, EMEA and the US, where uptime, scalability, security and performance directly impact customer experience and business growth.

You will play a key role in managing and evolving our multi-region AWS infrastructure, ensuring the platform remains highly available, resilient and secure as we continue to scale globally. Working closely with Engineering, QA and Product teams, you’ll help drive operational excellence across automation, observability, disaster recovery and cloud optimisation.

This is a hands-on role suited to someone who thrives in high-availability SaaS environments, enjoys solving complex infrastructure challenges and takes pride in building reliable, modern cloud platforms at scale.

Role Responsibilities

Design, deploy and maintain scalable, secure and highly available AWS infrastructure across multiple geographic regions including; APAC, EMEA and the US.

Build and maintain automated cloud environments using Infrastructure as Code (IaC).

Design and manage Infrastructure as Code using Terraform

Own disaster recovery, backup and high-availability strategies focused on maximising platform uptime and reliability.

Manage and optimise containerised workloads and orchestration platforms using Docker and Kubernetes.

Ensure security best practices for our AWS services; conducting regular security assessments and audits of cloud environments.

Collaborate with the Technology and QA Teams to resolve cross-functional issues.

What we’re looking for?

Minimum of 3 years hands-on AWS Cloud Engineer or SRE experience in a commercial, SaaS environment. (Essential)

Deep expertise designing, deploying and managing scalable AWS cloud infrastructure and services, including and not limited to; EC2/ECS/EKS, RDS/Aurora, Route 53, CloudFront, ELB/ALB/NLB, VPC networking, IAM, S3, Lambda and CloudWatch.

Strong experience with:

Infrastructure as Code (Terraform preferred)

Monitoring and observability platforms

Disaster recovery planning

Incident management

Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, Go or similar)

Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving capability

Experience supporting compliance frameworks such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI or similar.

Why Join MVSI?

First and foremost, fun people, fun culture

Work on a globally deployed AWS platform supporting a mission-critical SaaS product

Be part of a collaborative and high-performing technology team

Influence the reliability, scalability and future architecture of a rapidly evolving platform

Ongoing training and development, exposure to emerging technologies and best practices.

Opportunity to make a real impact in a growing technology business with enterprise customers worldwide

And we have snacks and drink fridges!

An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Data Engineering Specialist to join a highly respected organisation in Melbourne, where your expertise will directly influence the ongoing development and optimisation of enterprise data solutions. This role is perfect for someone who thrives on supporting business transformation through robust data engineering practices, ensuring that data-driven decision-making is at the heart of organisational success.

What you'll do:

As a Data Engineering Specialist based in Melbourne, you will immerse yourself in a variety of engaging projects that underpin the organisation's commitment to leveraging enterprise data for business efficiency. Your day-to-day activities will involve collaborating with diverse teams to develop innovative solutions for the ongoing enhancement of the data warehouse. You will be responsible for designing reliable ETL pipelines, implementing rigorous testing protocols, optimising analytical databases, and ensuring seamless integration between various business intelligence tools.

Provide comprehensive technical and strategic advice to senior management, staff, and stakeholders regarding all aspects of the organisation's data warehouse.
Develop and maintain robust ETL processes using Microsoft SSIS to ensure efficient loading of the data warehouse.
Design, implement, and oversee automated integration tests for all data warehouse operations to guarantee reliability and accuracy.
Create, manage, and optimise analysis services databases and cubes (SSAS), supporting advanced analytics requirements.

What you bring:

To excel as a Senior Data Engineer in this organisation, your background should reflect significant hands-on experience across multiple facets of enterprise-level data management.

Advanced Azure Data Factory (ADF) skills
Demonstrate extensive experience in database design or integration with SQL Server databases that enables you to architect scalable solutions tailored to organisational needs.
Possess advanced skills in relational database management systems (RDBMS) including proficiency with SQL language for querying complex datasets efficiently.
Exhibit deep understanding of Extraction, Transformation and Loading (ETL) processes using Microsoft SSIS which allows you to build reliable pipelines for large-scale data movement.

Robert Walters Australia is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff  Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
Tailored career development planning (including Education Assistance Program)

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

Job Description

The team

The AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Our AI Platform Engineering teams provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and deliver intelligent experiences at scale.

Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads operating within an APAC matrix organisational structure. We work in an Agile environment with regular standups, sprint ceremonies, and collaborative ways of working.

About the role 

As a Senior Staff Engineer at SEEK, you'll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you can make a real difference. You'll work on complex challenges that have a direct impact on people's lives, helping us evolve our business and reach new heights.

This role is a senior technical specialist within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams, focused on AI data infrastructure. Reporting to the Head of Engineering, you will be the authority on centralised data platforms for AI/ML workloads, proactively understanding the problems across our AI teams, developing the strategy, advocating for solutions, and delivering outcomes that accelerate AI innovation across SEEK.

Responsibilities 

Define and own the technical strategy for centralised AI data infrastructure across AIPS
Design scalable data architectures for AI/ML workloads spanning ingestion, transformation, and serving
Consult with our AI teams to deeply understand data needs and translate them into strategic platform solutions
Drive data governance, quality, and consistency across AI/ML data pipelines
Guide Staff Engineers and engineering teams on AI engineering best practices and patterns
Partner closely with Architecture, Data Services, and Security on data platform decisions
Advocate for and embed data engineering practices that reduce duplication and increase self-service capability
Support the Head of Engineering in capacity planning, scoping, and prioritisation of AI platform work, while contributing to the broader AIPS engineering community through knowledge sharing and technical leadership
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Experience delivering data solutions to real-time ML systems, with a strong understanding of how data engineering overlaps with ML engineering
Deep data engineering experience across pipelines, data modelling, feature engineering, and data governance at scale
Strong infrastructure experience with data-focused services and Infrastructure-as-Code practices
Python proficiency for data platform tooling, pipelines, and integrations
Demonstrated experience defining and implementing technical strategy that drives cultural and organisational change in the data space, underpinned by a strong understanding of MLOps and the ML lifecycle
Feature store and vector store implementation experience (or a demonstrated desire to build expertise in this space), along with MLOps or ML lifecycle experience spanning experiment tracking, model deployment, and training-serving consistency
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and cloud-agnostic data technologies, building self-service data capabilities for technical teams, and SRE or operational excellence experience for data platform systems

About the role 

As a Lead Engineer at SEEK, you'll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you can make a real difference. You'll work on complex challenges that have a direct impact on people's lives, helping us evolve our business and reach new heights.

This role provides technical and people leadership within our AI Platform Engineering domain — managing, mentoring, and developing the engineers who build the platforms powering AI innovation across SEEK. Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you will partner closely with the Staff Platform Engineer on technical direction and collaborate with Data Scientists, ML Engineers, and product teams to deliver scalable, resilient AI platform solutions. You will have 3–6 direct reports.

Responsibilities 

Lead and develop a high-performing team through coaching, mentoring, career development, and a culture of safety, inclusivity, and engagement
Drive delivery of AI platform systems from development through to production, ensuring high availability, resilience, and performance for production AI workloads
Contribute to technical strategy for GenAI and ML platforms in close partnership with the Staff Platform Engineer
Build and operate scalable AI infrastructure on AWS, leveraging Python and Infrastructure-as-Code
Strengthen engineering practices and DevOps culture for AI platform operations, including incident management, on-call processes, and supporting documentation
Build strong relationships with key stakeholders including Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Architecture, and Security
Lead recruitment, onboarding, and retention of engineering talent

Lead Data Scientist

We are hiring a Lead Data Scientist to own and build the data science function from the ground up. This is a hands-on, high-impact role focused on turning complex product, customer and operational data into measurable business value.
This is not a strategy-only position. You will design the approach, build the models, engineer pipelines, create insights, and establish the data practices that support a growing AI-powered platform. You will work closely with engineering, product, customer success, operations, finance and leadership to define what data means across the business and how it can drive better decisions.
About the role As the first dedicated data science hire, you will have the opportunity to shape the company’s data strategy, build the foundations of a modern data stack, and create the models, dashboards and reporting frameworks that teams rely on.
You will work across machine learning, applied AI, data engineering, analytics, governance and business intelligence. The role is suited to someone who is highly technical, commercially minded and comfortable operating in a fast-moving environment where they can build from scratch.

Key responsibilities
Design, build and deploy machine learning models and statistical analyses that solve real business problems
Work hands-on with LLM and RAG system data, including retrieval quality, conversation outcomes, latency and model performance
Build experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing and causal inference, to validate product and business decisions
Develop NLP and text analytics capability to extract insights from conversation data at scale
Architect scalable data pipelines across cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP or Azure
Build and maintain a modern data stack across ingestion, transformation, warehousing, orchestration and data quality
Establish data governance practices including cataloguing, lineage, access controls and compliance frameworks
Create dashboards, scorecards and executive reporting across product, engineering, customer success, finance, operations and leadership
Define key metrics across customer experience, product reliability, unit economics and operational health
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear, measurable data problems
Build the business case for growing the data function and hire or mentor future team members as needed
What you will bring
8–10+ years’ experience across data science, data engineering, analytics or applied AI/ML
Proven experience building and deploying machine learning models in production
Strong Python and SQL capability
Experience with modern data tooling such as dbt, Airflow, Dagster, Spark, Snowflake, BigQuery or Redshift
Experience architecting cloud-native data platforms across AWS, GCP or Azure
Hands-on experience with LLM-based systems, RAG architectures, vector databases and orchestration frameworks
Strong statistical foundations across experimental design, causal inference, regression, classification and time-series analysis
Experience with data governance, privacy and compliance in enterprise or regulated environments
Ability to communicate complex data insights to senior stakeholders and non-technical audiences
Experience in SaaS, AI/ML product companies or enterprise implementation environments
Nice to have
Experience working in the Australian market and familiarity with Australian privacy legislation
Familiarity with conversational AI metrics such as deflection, containment, intent accuracy and ASR/TTS performance
MLOps experience across model versioning, monitoring and CI/CD for ML pipelines
A quantitative degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or similar
Cloud or data certifications such as AWS Solutions Architect or GCP Professional Data Engineer
What success looks like In the first 30 days, you will complete a deep immersion into the product, data landscape and AI architecture, assess the current state of data, and deliver a clear roadmap covering risks, quick wins, metrics and priorities.
Over the following months, you will strengthen core data platform components, launch operational scorecards, improve data quality and model performance, and establish reporting rhythms across the business.
Over 6–12 months, you will have built a trusted data foundation, improved business outcomes through data science, matured metric ownership, and developed a clear plan for growing the data function.
Benefits
Join a fast-growing, innovative technology environment
Build and lead a data function from the ground up
Work at the forefront of AI, data science and conversational technology
High level of ownership, autonomy and business impact
Strong career growth and professional development opportunities
Competitive salary
Flexible, remote-first working environment

About the Role

Not every data engineering role gives you the chance to build the engine room.

This one does.

We’re partnering with a fast-growing Australian technology company that’s transforming a traditional industry through AI, automation, and data-driven products. With strong product-market fit, growing enterprise customers, and ambitious expansion plans, they’re now looking for a Senior Data & Analytics Engineer to take ownership of their modern data platform.

This is not a “maintain the dashboards” kind of role.

You’ll sit at the centre of a business where data powers:


real-time operational workflows,


AI-driven automation,


customer analytics,


computer vision models,


document intelligence,


and next-generation AI products.


The foundations are already in place - Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran, AWS - but the business has reached the point where they need someone senior enough to evolve the architecture, modernise pipelines, and drive the next phase of scale.

That’s where you come in.

The Opportunity You’ll join a small, highly collaborative Data & AI team in a genuinely high-ownership role.

You won’t inherit layers of process, large support teams, or narrowly defined responsibilities.

Instead, you’ll become the owner of the company’s “Data Hub” - the core platform powering everything from customer-facing reporting through to AI model training and real-time automation workflows.

One day you might be:

redesigning real-time data pipelines,


optimising Snowflake and ETL architecture,


improving governance and access controls,


enabling natural-language analytics experiences,


or building pipelines for OCR, PDF extraction, and computer vision workloads.

Why This Role Is Different The business is specifically looking for someone who can think strategically while remaining deeply hands-on.

You’ll have the autonomy to:

shape architectural direction,


influence platform strategy,


solve real business problems,


and build solutions yourself.


This is a role for someone who enjoys ownership and wants their work to directly influence product capability, AI innovation, and business growth.

Salary: Be part of a significant Defence program
Ad Text:
About us

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world's most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.
 

About the program
AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.


About the role

Take your career to new heights with Lockheed Martin Australia as a System Safety Engineer on the AIR6500 T2B FADS program, a next-generation defence capability that will transform Australia’s air and missile defence. In this key role, you'll help shape system safety outcomes across complex and critical technologies.

What you’ll do
Working under the guidance of the System Safety Lead, you will:

Assess system designs and procedures to ensure safety requirements are well defined and maintained

Conduct hazard analyses and safety risk assessments independently

Develop technical documentation to support system certification and test readiness

Collaborate closely with subject matter experts across engineering disciplines, including ILS, HSI, software, and cyber

Contribute to technical investigations, planning, scheduling, and estimation of safety engineering tasks

This role offers location flexibility and can be performed from either Adelaide, Canberra, Melbourne or Newcastle.

 

What you bring
We’re looking for someone with a sharp analytical mindset and a collaborative approach. Ideally, you will have: 

Strong experience in System Safety Engineering in Defence or a similar complex environment

Familiarity with safety standards like MIL-STD-882E and a solid understanding of the Systems Engineering lifecycle

Proven ability to analyse technical risks and produce clear, high-quality safety documentation

Knowledge of safety and risk management frameworks

Strong communication skills and the ability to work cross-functionally

Eligibility for or possession of an Australian NV-1 security clearance and ability to work with Export Controlled material 

Bonus points for:

Experience with Model-Based Systems Development (MBSD)

A track record of working effectively with diverse stakeholder groups 

Join a high-performing team where your work will have national impact, and your development will be supported every step of the way.


How We Reward and Support You

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of flexible work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs


Special Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees' unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.
